name: MIL-STD-1553 Avionics Bus
description: Military standard 1553 serial multiplex data bus used extensively in avionics, weapons systems, and command/control equipment. Supports up to 31 remote terminals on a single bus with deterministic real-time performance.

name: MIL-STD-1760 Weapons Store Interface
description: Military standard defining the electrical and functional interface for weapons, fuel tanks, and external stores on military aircraft. Enables ordnance management and release control.

name: MIL-STD-188-110 HF Waveform
description: Military standard for HF (High Frequency) digital modulation waveforms used in long-range strategic and tactical communications. Supports secure, frequency-hopping operation.

name: MIL-STD-188-141 HF Network Protocol
description: HF network protocol defining network layer and routing for military HF communications, supporting EMCON (emission control) and netted operations.

name: Link 16 JTIDS
description: NATO tactical data link providing secure, jam-resistant, real-time tactical information exchange. Uses frequency hopping and spot beam technology for anti-jam resilience.

name: Link 11 TADIL-A
description: Legacy NATO tactical data link (TADIL-A) providing basic tactical data exchange for air defense and C2 systems. Predecessor to Link 16.

name: Link 22 TADIL-J NATO
description: NATO improved tactical data link offering enhanced security and interoperability over Link 11. Supports digital voice and legacy data formats with improved error correction.

name: JREAP-C JTRS Extension
description: Joint Range Extension Applications Protocol for JTRS radios, extending interoperability across military platforms with waveform-agnostic networking.

name: SINCGARS VHF Radio
description: Single Channel Ground and Airborne Radio System providing secure, frequency-hopping VHF voice and data communications for tactical military operations.

name: JTRS Software Configurable Architecture
description: Software-defined radio architecture enabling rapid waveform swapping and multi-mode operation across military and tactical platforms. Supports 40+ waveforms.

name: MUOS Mobile User Objective System
description: Military satellite communication system providing secure, mobile SATCOM with improved capacity and coverage over legacy systems. Integrated with tactical networks.

name: AN/PRC-117G Manpack Radio
description: Portable military radio system providing multi-waveform, multi-band operation for tactical field communications. Supports voice, data, and narrowband operations.

name: AN/PRC-152A Handheld Radio
description: Handheld military radio offering secure voice and data communications in compact form factor. Common in special operations and small unit operations.

name: BOWMAN UK Military Network
description: British military tactical communications system integrating radio, data, and positioning for land force operations. Replaced by more modern systems.

name: SIPRNet Secret Internet Protocol Network
description: Classified IP network for secret-level US military and intelligence communications. Air-gapped from unclassified networks with dedicated encryption.

name: NIPRNet Unclassified IP Network
description: Unclassified but restricted US military IP network for official use. Connected to internet through controlled gateways with monitoring and filtering.

name: JWICS Top Secret Compartmented Network
description: Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmented Information intelligence network used by military and IC agencies. Highest classification level network infrastructure.

name: DAMA Demand Assigned Multiple Access
description: Satellite uplink protocol providing dynamic channel assignment for tactical SATCOM operations. Supports efficient spectrum utilization on shared satellite transponders.

name: Wideband Global SATCOM WGS
description: Military satellite constellation providing broadband SATCOM to worldwide platforms. Supports video, data, and voice with multiple spot beams.

name: AEHF Advanced Extremely High Frequency
description: Ultra-secure military SATCOM providing protected communications for strategic and operational command authorities. Uses EHF band with advanced anti-jam coding.

name: Tactical Internet Network
description: IP-based tactical network providing layered data and voice communications for army operations. Early military tactical IP implementation.

name: WIN-T Warfighter Information Network Tactical
description: Modern US Army tactical network providing integrated broadband communications to brigade and battalion level. Supports video, voice, and real-time data.

name: FBCB2 Force XXI Battle Command
description: Army digital command and control system providing real-time position and status of friendly and enemy forces. Layered on Tactical Internet.

name: EPLRS Enhanced Position Location Reporting
description: Secure frequency-hopping tactical data link for position reporting and limited data exchange. Used in air defense and air operations.

name: SADL Situation Awareness Data Link
description: Airborne tactical data link for situation awareness among helicopters and ground units. Low-bandwidth secure link for position and status.

name: IFDL Intra-Flight Data Link
description: High-bandwidth tactical data link for air-to-air combat data exchange among fighter aircraft in formation. Supports real-time track and targeting data.

name: MADL Multifunction Advanced Data Link F-35
description: Advanced data link for F-35 providing secure, low-probability-of-intercept communications with other F-35s and legacy platforms. Critical for net-centric warfare.

name: CDL Common Data Link
description: Military standard data link for real-time transmission of intelligence imagery and sensor data from reconnaissance platforms to ground stations.

name: TCDL Tactical Common Data Link
description: Tactical variant of CDL supporting encrypted multicast transmission of imagery and sensor data for real-time tactical operations.

name: TTNT Tactical Targeting Network Technology
description: Real-time targeting data distribution network enabling rapid threat detection and engagement coordination. Supports video and track distribution.

name: HCLOS High Capacity Line of Sight
description: Military microwave point-to-point link providing high-bandwidth secure communications between fixed and mobile tactical operations centers.

name: ANW2 Adaptive Networking Wideband Waveform
description: Software-defined waveform enabling adaptive bandwidth and modulation for JTRS platforms. Supports MANET routing and frequency-hopping.

name: SRW Soldier Radio Waveform
description: Optimized SDR waveform for soldier-level portable radios. Provides reliable squad and platoon-level communications with efficient spectrum usage.

name: WNW Wideband Networking Waveform
description: Higher-bandwidth networking waveform for JTRS enabling IP-based tactical networking with mobile ad-hoc routing.

name: TSM Tactical Scalable MANET
description: Mobile ad-hoc networking protocol providing self-healing mesh routing for tactical operations without infrastructure.

name: DISA Transport Protocol
description: Defense Information Systems Agency standardized transport layer for military networks. Supports quality-of-service and priority delivery.

name: NATO STANAG Protocols
description: Standardization agreements defining interoperable protocols for NATO communications and systems. Covers tactical, strategic, and technical standards.

name: Fibre Channel Military Ruggedized
description: Military-grade Fibre Channel implementation for high-speed storage networking in tactical environments with environmental hardening.

name: ELF/VLF Submarine Communication
description: Extremely low frequency and very low frequency systems for strategic submarine communications. World-spanning coverage with inherent stealth.

name: Troposcatter Long-Range Communication
description: Over-the-horizon radio communications using troposphere scatter propagation. Provides beyond line-of-sight coverage for strategic communications.

name: CAN Bus Controller Area Network
description: Industry-standard vehicle databus enabling ECU communication with collision detection and arbitration. Dominant in automotive for 30+ years.

name: CAN FD Flexible Data-Rate
description: Extended CAN supporting variable frame size and higher speeds up to 5 Mbps. Enables richer payloads for modern vehicle functions.

name: CAN XL Extended Length
description: Latest CAN evolution supporting 32-byte payloads and 10 Mbps speeds. Designed for video and advanced driver assistance systems.

name: LIN Bus Local Interconnect Network
description: Low-cost single-wire bus for non-critical vehicle functions. Used for body electronics, lighting, and low-speed sensor networks.

name: FlexRay Deterministic Bus
description: Time-triggered deterministic bus for safety-critical functions. Used in drive-by-wire and active safety systems with dual-channel redundancy.

name: MOST Media Oriented Systems Transport
description: Optical/electrical bus for multimedia and infotainment in vehicles. Supports synchronous and asynchronous data with isochronous video streaming.

name: MOST25 Infotainment Bus
description: First generation MOST operating at 25 Mbps. Early automotive entertainment and navigation network standard.

name: MOST50 Enhanced Infotainment
description: Second generation MOST doubling bandwidth to 50 Mbps. Supports HD video streaming and high-quality audio for premium vehicles.

name: MOST150 High-Bandwidth Network
description: Third generation MOST supporting 150 Mbps. Enables 4K video and advanced autonomous driving sensor distribution.

name: Automotive Ethernet 100BASE-T1
description: Single twisted-pair Ethernet for automotive at 100 Mbps. Replaces heavy multi-pair cables with unified Ethernet infrastructure.

name: Automotive Ethernet 1000BASE-T1
description: Gigabit single-pair Ethernet for automotive. Supports high-bandwidth autonomous driving sensor fusion and software updates.

name: 10BASE-T1S Multidrop Automotive
description: 10 Mbps single-pair Ethernet supporting multidrop topology. Enables star-free network architectures with minimal cabling.

name: BroadR-Reach Automotive Ethernet
description: Earlier proprietary automotive Ethernet variant supporting 100 Mbps on single twisted pair. Predecessor to standardized 100BASE-T1.

name: SENT Single Edge Nibble Transmission
description: Simple single-wire sensor protocol for analog value transmission. Used for environmental sensors with inherent error checking.

name: PSI5 Peripheral Sensor Interface
description: Two-wire sensor interface supporting asynchronous and synchronous transmission. Common for pressure and temperature sensors.

name: DSI3 Distributed Systems Interface
description: Three-wire sensor interface supporting digital and analog transmission. Used for advanced sensor clusters with diagnostics.

name: CXPI Clock Extension Peripheral Interface
description: Single-wire sensor interface with integrated clock and power delivery. Modern replacement for SENT with bidirectional capability.

name: A2B Automotive Audio Bus
description: Two-wire digital audio bus for automotive replacing analog audio distribution. Supports multiple high-quality audio streams with networking.

name: C-V2X Cellular Vehicle-to-Everything
description: Cellular-based V2X using LTE/5G for vehicle communication. Supports long-range connectivity and infrastructure integration.

name: DSRC Dedicated Short Range Communications
description: Dedicated 5.9 GHz spectrum for vehicle-to-vehicle and vehicle-to-infrastructure communications. Legacy V2X standard being phased out.

name: V2X Vehicle-to-Everything
description: Umbrella term for vehicle communication with infrastructure, pedestrians, and network. Includes both C-V2X and DSRC implementations.

name: V2V Vehicle-to-Vehicle Communication
description: Direct vehicle-to-vehicle wireless communication for collision avoidance and cooperative driving. Typically DSRC or C-V2X based.

name: V2I Vehicle-to-Infrastructure
description: Communication between vehicles and roadside infrastructure for cooperative traffic management and safety services.

name: OBD-II On-Board Diagnostics
description: Standardized diagnostic interface for emission control systems. Provides engine fault codes and real-time emissions data.

name: UDS Unified Diagnostic Services
description: Modern protocol for vehicle diagnostics, programming, and testing. Supports ECU identification, memory access, and reprogramming.

name: DoIP Diagnostics over IP
description: Diagnostics protocol over Ethernet/IP networks. Enables telematics-based vehicle diagnostics and remote servicing.

name: SOME/IP Scalable Service-Oriented Middleware
description: Service-oriented middleware for automotive systems. Enables distributed ECU architecture with dynamic service discovery.

name: DDS Data Distribution Service Automotive
description: Real-time data distribution middleware for autonomous driving systems. Supports low-latency sensor fusion across ECUs.

name: AUTOSAR Ethernet Architecture
description: AUTOSAR standardized Ethernet stack for vehicle electronics. Defines abstraction layers for hardware-independent ECU development.

name: SAE J1939 Heavy Duty Protocol
description: CAN-based protocol for heavy-duty trucks and off-highway vehicles. Standardizes parameter groups for engine and drivetrain.

name: SAE J1708/J1587 Legacy Heavy Duty
description: Legacy serial protocol for heavy-duty vehicles. Predecessor to J1939 with simpler message structure.

name: ISO 11783 ISOBUS Agricultural
description: CAN-based network for agricultural machinery. Standardizes communication between tractor and implements.

name: NMEA 2000 Marine Network
description: CAN-based marine electronics network. Also used in some automotive aftermarket applications for data logging.

name: Tesla CAN Architecture
description: Proprietary CAN-based vehicle network used in Tesla electric vehicles. Supports advanced battery management and autonomous features.

name: Rivian Ethernet Backbone
description: Modern Ethernet-based vehicle architecture for Rivian electric vehicles. Supports high-bandwidth autonomous driving and infotainment.

name: Aptiv Smart Architecture
description: Modular vehicle architecture platform supporting multiple communication buses and interfaces. Used by tier-1 suppliers.

name: Zonal Architecture Ethernet
description: Next-generation vehicle architecture using zonal gateways and Ethernet backbone. Reduces wiring harness complexity.

name: DICOM Network Medical Imaging
description: Standard medical imaging communication protocol for diagnostic equipment. Enables image transfer and archival across hospital networks.

name: HL7 v2 Health Level 7
description: Standard protocol for healthcare information exchange between hospital systems. Supports ADT, orders, results, and billing.

name: HL7 FHIR Fast Healthcare Interoperability
description: Modern healthcare data exchange standard using RESTful APIs and JSON. Enables interoperability across cloud and mobile platforms.

name: IEEE 11073 Medical Device Communication
description: Standard for medical device communication including vitals monitors, pumps, and ventilators. Enables plug-and-play medical device integration.

name: IEEE 11073-20601 Personal Health Devices
description: Standard for wearable and portable medical devices. Enables wireless health monitoring and telemedicine applications.

name: IHE XDS Cross-Enterprise Document Sharing
description: Framework for secure sharing of clinical documents across healthcare organizations. Enables patient data portability.

name: Continua Health Alliance
description: Industry alliance protocols for personal health device connectivity. Standards for weight scales, glucose monitors, and activity trackers.

name: Bluetooth Low Energy Medical Profiles
description: BLE profiles for medical device communication including heart rate, blood pressure, and glucose monitoring.

name: ANT+ Medical Devices
description: ANT+ protocol for medical wearables and fitness devices. Low-power wireless for continuous health monitoring.

name: Medical Body Area Network MBAN
description: Ultra-short range wireless for on-body medical sensors. FDA-approved spectrum for patient monitoring networks.

name: WMTS Wireless Medical Telemetry Service
description: Licensed spectrum for hospital-grade wireless medical telemetry. Supports continuous patient monitoring with interference protection.

name: ISM Band Medical Devices
description: 2.4 GHz ISM band devices for medical applications including wireless pulse oximetry and patient monitors.

name: MedRadio Medical Device Radio
description: FCC-licensed spectrum for implantable and body-worn medical devices. Provides reliable long-range communication.

name: MICS Medical Implant Communication Service
description: Dedicated spectrum for implantable medical devices. Enables wireless monitoring of pacemakers and defibrillators.

name: Zigbee Health Care Profile
description: Zigbee profile for healthcare IoT devices. Low-power mesh networking for hospital automation and monitoring.

name: NFC Near Field Communication Medical
description: NFC for medical device pairing and patient identification. Ultra-short range contactless communication.

name: UWB Medical Device Ranging
description: Ultra-wideband for precise positioning and ranging of mobile medical devices. Emerging for surgical robot localization.

name: Hospital WiFi 802.11 Medical Grade
description: Hospital-grade 802.11 networks with medical device support and interference monitoring. QoS for critical medical applications.

name: Nurse Call Systems Network
description: Hospital network for patient-initiated and clinical alerts. Integrates with bed units and mobile stations.

name: Patient Monitoring Network
description: Networked patient monitoring system combining bedside monitors, central station, and electronic health records.

name: Surgical Robot Communication Link
description: Real-time communication link for robotic surgery systems with redundancy and low-latency guarantees.

name: Telemedicine Communication Link
description: Secure video and data link for remote patient consultations and remote diagnosis support.

name: Medical IoT Gateway
description: Gateway aggregating medical IoT devices for hospital networks. Protocol translation and data normalization.

name: Pharmacy Automation Network
description: Network for automated medication dispensing and tracking systems. Integrates with inventory and EHR systems.

name: Lab Information System Network LIS
description: Network for laboratory test ordering, result reporting, and instrument interfacing.

name: PACS Picture Archiving and Communication
description: Hospital imaging network for storing, retrieving, and transmitting medical images. Integrated with DICOM systems.

name: RIS Radiology Information System Network
description: Network for radiology workflow including scheduling, reporting, and quality control.

name: Clinical Decision Support Network
description: Network supporting real-time clinical decision support at point of care. Integrates with EHR for evidence-based alerts.

name: Medical Gas Monitoring Network
description: Network for monitoring and alarm reporting of medical gas systems in hospital infrastructure.

name: Infusion Pump Network
description: Network for smart infusion pump integration with EHR and medication administration systems.

name: Ventilator Communication Network
description: Network for ventilator connectivity to monitoring systems and central station for critical care coordination.

name: Implantable Device Telemetry
description: Wireless telemetry for remote monitoring of implantable devices including pacemakers and defibrillators.
